items.title: Astellas’ consolidation efforts to impact 50 Universal Cells employees
items.snippet: Follow along as BioSpace tracks job cuts and restructuring initiatives.

items.title: Lilly’s Foundayo reaches 1,390 patients in first week, trailing Novo’s oral Wegovy launch
items.snippet: While Novo Nordisk’s Wegovy pill reached more than 3,000 patients in its first week on the market, analysts at RBC Capital Markets said a direct comparison of the two figures could be misleading given the shorter data collection time for Foundayo.

items.title: UPDATE: Kailera breaks biotech IPO record with $625M debut
items.snippet: Obesity-focused Kailera Therapeutics debuted on the Nasdaq Friday after raising a record $625 million, beating Moderna’s $600 million from 2018.

items.title: RFK Jr. defends Makary, claims pharma ‘owns’ Congress and media
items.snippet: The pharma industry “own Congress, they own the media,” Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy, Jr. told lawmakers by way of explaining the bad press against FDA Commissioner Marty Makary following the second rejection of Replimune’s advanced melanoma drug.

items.title: Novo may have muscle advantage over Lilly in weight-loss race: preprint
items.snippet: More patients on Eli Lilly’s tirzepatide lost over 5% of their lean mass versus those on Novo Nordisk’s semaglutide, according to a study that has yet to be peer reviewed.

items.title: Replimune cries foul on regulatory flexibility. But many Americans want a stricter FDA
items.snippet: After Replimune’s advanced melanoma drug was rejected for a second time, CEO Sushil Patel slammed the FDA for failing to exercise regulatory flexibility, while other experts bemoaned the agency’s lack of consistency. With new safety guidelines for gene editing therapies, the FDA has taken a first step toward fixing bo...

items.title: Merck’s PD-1/VEGF data star in stacked lineup of AACR ‘26 data reveals
items.snippet: The Merck update, which will shed light on a $588 million bet to succeed Keytruda, is part of a roster of presentations that could shape the future of ADCs, protein degraders and KRAS-targeted therapies.

items.title: Funding the future of European biotech
items.snippet: In this episode of Denatured, you'll be hearing from Edoardo Negroni, co-founder & managing partner at AurorA-TT and Naveed Siddiqi, senior partner, Venture Investments at Novo Holdings. We debate whether Europe’s world-class science can be matched by a truly integrated venture ecosystem—and what it would take, in pra...

items.title: Trump nominates new CDC director, appoints various leaders to embattled agency
items.snippet: Erica Schwartz, a former deputy surgeon general and member of the U.S. military, will take over from what has amounted to a rapid succession of CDC leaders over the past year.

items.title: Drug pricing watchdog calls for increased transparency into FDA’s accelerated approval decisions
items.snippet: Greater transparency in FDA decision-making was possibly the most common theme for stakeholders interviewed by the Institute for Clinical and Economic Review in a new report focused on strengthening the agency’s accelerated approval program.

items.title: FDA mulls compounding for peptides previously flagged over safety risks
items.snippet: With Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. declaring himself “a big fan of peptides,” the FDA has arranged an advisory committee to discuss allowing compounders to make molecules despite earlier safety concerns.

items.title: MeiraGTx, spying hope in a failed trial, buys back eye disease gene therapy from J&J
items.snippet: J&J will hand over the rights to bota-vec for $25 million upfront, clearing MeiraGTx to seek regulatory approvals in the U.S. and EU in 2027.

items.title: Lilly’s Foundayo posts ‘outstanding’ diabetes data, possibly easing FDA safety concerns
items.snippet: Right after the FDA flagged safety concerns for Eli Lilly’s newly approved obesity pill, the pharma posted diabetes data demonstrating a clean safety profile and revealed plans to seek approval in the indication.

items.title: Can Europe turn world-class science into world-class scale?
items.snippet: Despite exceptional regional hubs and research strength, investors say Europe still needs more integrated incubators, smarter regulation and broader pools of patient capital to keep breakthrough companies growing at home.

items.title: Obesity’s explosive growth continues as Q1 deals exceed total 2025 value
items.snippet: Commitments in obesity- and diabetes-focused deals reached $22 billion in the first quarter of 2026—already eclipsing last year’s total of $20.3 billion, according to a new J.P. Morgan report.

items.title: Revolution doubles stock offering target to $2B following pancreatic cancer win
items.snippet: After Revolution Medicines’ drug candidate doubled survival in a Phase 3 pancreatic cancer study, the biotech is hoping to attract more investor money, with new plans for a $2 billion stock and debt offering.

items.title: Novartis CEO joins Anthropic board, embedding AI in the heart of biopharma
items.snippet: In recent months, Anthropic has been building more and more ties with the biopharma industry, including partnerships with Big Pharma companies such as Sanofi, Novo Nordisk and AbbVie.

items.title: Biopharmas pull back on layoffs in Q1
items.snippet: The trend of fewer companies letting employees go, which began in the fourth quarter of last year, has continued. However, more employees were on the chopping block in Q1 2026 than in Q1 2025, due mainly to one company’s cuts.

items.title: Whitepaper: CDMOs at a Crossroads
items.snippet: In this whitepaper, BioSpace reviews the major trends impacting the CDMO sector and the evolving relationship between sponsors and providers. We examine the key qualities pharma and biotech should consider in CDMO selection, and how the macroeconomic and macrodevelopment factors affecting the space play a role in this...

items.title: BMS makes a Beeline, bringing 5 assets to biotech's $300M precision immunology debut
items.snippet: Leading Beeline Medicines’ pipeline is afimetoran, which is in Phase 2 development for systemic lupus erythematosus with data expected later this year.

items.title: Replimune cries foul on regulatory flexibility. But many Americans want a stricter FDA
items.snippet: After Replimune’s advanced melanoma drug was rejected for a second time, CEO Sushil Patel slammed the FDA for failing to exercise regulatory flexibility, while other experts bemoaned the agency’s lack of consistency. With new safety guidelines for gene editing therapies, the FDA has taken a first step toward fixing bo...

items.title: With oral obesity race underway, all eyes are on Lilly’s Foundayo launch
items.snippet: Novo Nordisk’s oral Wegovy has a few months’ head start on Eli Lilly’s newly approved pill. While the Indianapolis pharma has come from behind the Danish rival in the weight loss space before, last time it clearly had the better drug.

items.title: Pharma's Reluctance To Serve Europe To Dodge MFN Drug Pricing Is Chilling
items.snippet: As Big Pharma companies consider foregoing European drug launches to avoid reducing drug prices in the U.S. in alignment with Trump’s Most Favored Nation policy, patients will suffer.

items.title: Prasad’s Request To Remain Anonymous Shines Light on FDA’s Transparency Problem
items.snippet: While requests by government officials for anonymity when speaking to the media are nothing new, the practice attracts more scrutiny when the Department for Health and Human Services has pledged a commitment to “radical transparency.”
